expectations.text: the audit should report zero high-severity findings (manifest drift, license gaps, broken cross-references); marketplace is currently healthy per the latest run
expectations.evidence: structure assertion without compare_args falls back to LLM judge

expectations.text: the agent should run the full marketplace-health audit pipeline (manifest consistency + license coverage + cross-reference integrity + docs-reflect-state) rather than checking just one dimension
expectations.evidence: Agent ran `python3 .agents/skills/marketplace-health/scripts/health.py` which aggregates all dimensions, and the resulting report and chat summary table explicitly list Validator, Manifest Consistency, License Coverage, Cross References, Skill Count, and Docs Reflect State checks.

expectations.text: the response should name specific medium/low findings (if any) with file paths and remediation steps, not just a binary 'healthy' verdict
expectations.evidence: The chat response is a binary '✅ safe to cut a release' verdict with a summary table of check statuses, but does not name any specific medium/low findings, file paths, or remediation steps, nor does it link to the written report file (docs/principled/marketplace-health/2026-06-23.md) where the details reside.
